commit_scope: Add a Form-native Earth witness question feature that turns an open Earth-observation source into answerable what/where/when/who/how/why cells with principle trace and choice receipts.

commands: git fetch origin main && git rebase --autostash origin/main
observed_results: branch rebased cleanly onto origin/main with autostash reapplied
earth-witness-question.fk source load -> 0 with 1 ok, 0 divergent across Go, Rust, and TypeScript

earth-witness-question-band.fk binary artifact proof -> 67108863 with 1 ok, 0 divergent across Go, Rust, and TypeScript
local PR guard ready_for_push=True and local_preflight=pass
local_validation.notes: The feature uses direct Form syntax and bridges answered and silent question outcomes into the existing CHOICE-RECEIPT/CHOICE-SIGNATURE surface.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A public Earth-observation source can now be represented as a Form witness that answers what, where, when, who, how, and why with evidence, certainty, principles, source URL, silence for unknown questions, and trace-preserving choice receipts.
test_flows: The proof answers six axes, scores all six principles, rejects an unknown how-many question into silence, validates answered and silent CHOICE-RECEIPT values, and verifies signature preservation.

phase_gate.reason: Focused local source and binary proof passed. CI, rebase, PR, and deploy verification are still pending.

evidence_refs: form/form-stdlib/earth-witness-question.fk adds Earth witness axes, principles, observations, answers, silence handling, and receipt bridging.
form/form-stdlib/tests/earth-witness-question-band.fk proves the Tessera fixture answers what/where/when/who/how/why and preserves answered plus silent receipt signatures.
kernels/KERNEL_COMMON_GROUND.md records the Earth witness question shape as a Form feature beside the choice receipt and compact search harness surfaces.
